free_text: Excitation function of Nb-93(He-3,xn)Tc-93-m,g,
Tc-94-m,g, Tc-95-m,g processes from threshold up to
35 MeV: possibility of production of Tc-94-m in high
radiochemical purity using a thermochromatographic
separation technique
.Institute fuer nuclearchemie
irradiation were done for 20
min or 90 min at 200 Na with 36 and 25 MeV incident
energy of He3 beam. Beam current was measured using a
faraday  cup and via monitor reactions on Ti. The
incident particle energies were checked by measuring
the distance between two beam bunches (Z. Kormany,
J,NIM,A337,258,94). The energy degradation along the
stack was calculated according to C.F.Williamson et
Al., Rapport CEA-R 3042 (1966). Decay data of reaction
product were taken from E. Brownie and R.B. Fierstone,
table of radioactive isotopes (V.S. Shirly, ed.) Wiley
London, 1986
10 stacks containing 8 to 10 of 5 and 10 microns of
Nb foils (chemical purity greater than 99.99%),
aluminum foils of 10 -100 microns as absorber and
titanium monitor foils (13 microns) were irradiated.
the foil diameter was 13 mm.
.Dead time < 5%, source detector
distance > 10 cm
experimental determination of Tc isotopes present as
impurities. Development of optimum conditions for a
thermochromatic separation of technetium from the
irradiated niobium target.
.The uncertainties of the energy scale was
estimated to increase from +- 0.2 Mev  to +- 0.5 Mev
with decreasing He-3 energy in the range of 36 MeV to
15 MeV.
.The total error of the cross sections was
derived as the square root of the sum of square of the
individual errors.
-target mass 0.1%
-irradiation 0.5%
-detector efficiency including geometry 5%
-decay data 0.5%
-excitation function of monitor reactions 8%
-counting statistics 1%-10%
-peak area analysis 2%-5%
the total error amounted to 50-100% near threshold in
the case of Tc-95-m,g and 30% for the other products,
BIB section upgrated
(by author).Direct production data. Contribution from
the decay of 43-Tc-93m was separated.
(by compiler). The separation of the contribution of
decay of Tc-93m : sigma(Tc-93d)=sigma(Tc-93m+g)-
0.8*Sigma(Tc-93m) is valid only at constant
(independent of He-3 energy) isomeric ratio which is
not supported by the obtained results.
(by author).The intensity of the 871 keV gamma-ray
emitted from the decay of the ground state was
separated on the basis of the different half life and
also on the measurements of the intensity of other
gamma-rays
(by author).The intensity of the 871 keV gamma-ray
emitted from the decay of the isomer state was
separated on the basis of the different half life and
also on the measurements of the intensity of other
gamma-rays
(by compiler).No comment was given by the author on
the separation of the isomeric state
